pm_bug_tickets.title: Boot crash: PauseMenu constructed before audio_system (AttributeError: GameEngine has no attribute audio_system)
repro_steps: Run: python main.py (or python main.py --no-llm / --provider mock)
pm_bug_tickets.expected: Game boots successfully; ESC menu can reference AudioSystem safely.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Crash: AttributeError: 'GameEngine' object has no attribute 'audio_system' when constructing PauseMenu.
evidence: Trace shows: game/engine.py calls PauseMenu(... audio_system=self.audio_system) before self.audio_system exists.
suspected_area: game/engine.py (GameEngine.__init__ initialization order)
game/ui/pause_menu.py (expects audio_system reference)
constraints: Keep settings/UI non-authoritative (no sim-state changes).
Keep determinism unchanged.
Avoid headless/dummy driver regressions if possible.
proposed_fix: Reorder GameEngine initialization so self.audio_system is created before PauseMenu is constructed.
If any UI construction needs window_width/height, ensure those are set before allocating surfaces (avoid init-order crashes).
Keep the change minimal and localized to engine init ordering.
acceptance_test: python main.py --no-llm boots to a running window (no crash)
python main.py --provider mock boots to a running window (no crash)
python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick PASS

pm_bug_tickets.title: Boot crash: apply_display_settings -> clamp_camera uses self.zoom before initialization (AttributeError: zoom)
evidence: Trace: game/engine.py __init__ calls apply_display_settings(); apply_display_settings calls clamp_camera(); clamp_camera reads self.zoom but GameEngine has no attribute zoom yet.

pm_bug_tickets.actual: Crash on boot: AttributeError for self.zoom.

apply_display_settings called too early in __init__ before zoom is set
constraints: No simulation determinism changes; UI/system-only.
Keep fix minimal and robust (no reliance on optional attributes).
proposed_fix: Initialize self.zoom (and any related camera vars used by clamp_camera) before calling apply_display_settings in __init__.

pm_bug_tickets.title: Boot crash: UITheme dataclass(slots=True) cannot set font_* attributes in __post_init__
repro_steps: Run: python main.py (or python main.py --no-llm / --provider mock)
Observe crash during GameEngine init when HUD creates UITheme
pm_bug_tickets.expected: Game boots successfully; UITheme initializes fonts safely.
pm_bug_tickets.actual: Crash: AttributeError: 'UITheme' object has no attribute 'font_title' and no __dict__ for setting new attributes
evidence: Trace points to game/ui/theme.py: UITheme.__post_init__ assigning self.font_title/body/small on a slots dataclass without declaring those fields.
suspected_area: game/ui/theme.py (UITheme dataclass)
game/ui/hud.py (constructs UITheme in HUD.__init__)
constraints: Keep behavior deterministic and perf-safe.
Prefer keeping slots=True; declare font fields properly (init=False) rather than removing slots unless necessary.
proposed_fix: In UITheme dataclass, add fields: font_title/font_body/font_small (e.g., pygame.font.Font | None) with init=False default=None, then assign in __post_init__.
Alternative: remove slots=True (less preferred).
acceptance_test: python main.py --no-llm boots to a running window (no crash)
python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick remains PASS
resolution_summary: Agent 08 kept `@dataclass(slots=True)` and declared `font_title/font_body/font_small` as dataclass fields (init=False, default=None), then assigned them in `UITheme.__post_init__`.
Boot verified in `--no-llm` and `--provider mock` and `python tools/qa_smoke.py --quick` PASS per Agent 08 log (wk3_r4).
resolution_evidence: Code: game/ui/theme.py declares the font fields using dataclasses.field(...) and assigns in __post_init__.

pm_bug_tickets.title: Teal/green square 'grass rug' artefacts scattered across meadow
repro_steps: python main.py --renderer ursina (once camera is fixed per BUG-008)
Pan over any meadow area. Large flat teal/green-tinted squares visible on ground.
pm_bug_tickets.expected: Grass scatter reads as natural foliage — varied small tufts, flowers, no visible flat quad silhouette.

pm_bug_tickets.pm_diagnosis_hints: Most likely cause: one or more GLB files promoted by Agent 15 are PLANAR QUADS (a flat mesh used as a 'sprite' in Kenney's Nature Kit) rather than 3D foliage geometry. When Ursina loads a planar quad with a texture and the 0.75 tint is applied as entity.color = rgb(0.75, 0.75, 0.75), the tinted texture can read as a flat colored rectangle at scatter scale. Candidate culprits (check these first): grass_tuft_a.glb, grass_tuft_b.glb, grass_flower_red.glb, grass_flower_yellow.glb — if these are texture-atlas quads rather than 3D geometry, they will show the alpha-silhouette problem. VISUAL ACCEPTANCE LOOP IS MANDATORY this time: 1. Launch python tools/model_viewer_kenney.py and find each grass/doodad model in the environment section. 2. Orbit the camera 90 degrees — if the model disappears edge-on, it is a flat quad. 3. Flat quads need billboard=True to look correct in scatter (or should be replaced with 3D geometry). OBJ-format models (rock_gy_cluster.obj etc.) are more likely to be true 3D and should be fine. 4. Screenshot every model from two angles (front + 90deg side) and log the results. 5. For any model that is a flat quad: either (a) set billboard=True in the scatter Entity spawned in ursina_renderer.py, or (b) replace with a 3D equivalent. Coordinate with Agent 03 if billboard flag needs adding to the scatter path.
acceptance_test: No flat square / 'rug' artefacts visible in meadow.
F12 screenshot from same camera angle as Jaimie's bug screenshot showing clean grass.
Ask verbatim: 'Does this look like real grass and nature?' Answer must be YES.
